comes to the product catalog and creating DPA campaigns dynamic product ad campaigns one of the cool things about what Facebook have enabled is the opportunity to create subsets of your product catalog what that might mean by that is let's say you're running a multi-product store and you've got multiple categories and products and things like that one of the common things that will occur is you may get products that are recommended that you don't necessarily focus on as your kind of key products and you know it could be for example you're selling products which are ranging in price from Facebook to serve those as retargeting ads your 10 15 $20 products because that's going to impact your average order value so what you can do for example is create product sets so on the left hand side here you can see the product sets and you can create that for any attribute that you can pull out of Facebook so an attribute could be a category name some some kind of naming in the product itself could be a variant it could be price as well and it enables you to create smaller product sets to create your DPA campaigns so that when you're running the retargeting ads you're telling Facebook to just focus in on these products an example use case is literally just saying only surface products that are above $30 for example because below that you don't really want that to be your focal focal product in your ad because the return on aspen's just not gonna be good enough so that's one of the things that I would consider using so for example on this store we can have a look at could be something you do manually or you use an external tool to pull your data from your e-commerce sales database Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, whatever it is into Facebook and you're probably ## [03:02 - 06:05]

example and we've manually added a few others as well so when you create your product set which you can do in the catalog manager within business manager you can you can search and add and you can also manually add products into your product set it could be you know and this is an example of a front-end product set you can also create back-end product sets related to what they've purchased this is another thing that is really cool that Facebook have created so for example if you're selling jeans for example at the front end and that's your kind of main offer or if you're selling jeans but you know that generally you've got products that go really well with the jeans like a jacket a shirt or something like that you can create a product set which only contains jackets for example and then target people that bought jeans in your ad set and you can do that as a cross-sell campaign post purchase so what that means is you know your front-end offer could be the jeans you could say you know you could have a promotion on it you could have just key messages whatever it is even if you break even even if you make a loss like let's say for example your jeans on average cost $80 and your cost per acquisition is $50 which means considering the cost of goods you're not really making that much profit but you know that when people buy the jeans hey they become loyal customers and be they likely to purchase again you could for example see that as a what we call a loss leader so it's a key product that you sell at the front of your funnel and then either through the e-com funnel on your site all through retargeting or through email you're going to get them to buy related products and the way that would work with Facebook is for example you'd create a product set of items that are related to that particular purchase that they've made so you might have a product set that contains everything but jeans could be shirts could be jackets socks shoes etc to say complete the look so you could then target people at the bottom of your full funnel system as existing customers and offer them maybe a discount on their repurchase for example and especially when they're existing customers they are more likely to buy again if they're happy and they're the cost per acquisition is going to be lower as well because you're not prospecting now these people know you don't need to take them through a journey of explaining your brand and why it's relevant and things like that combining retargeting ads with email flows with post purchase funnels to get people to buy more is one of the ways to drive scalability and profitability not just in e-com in info products as well doesn't matter what is but you it's that thing about understanding that the first acquisition is your cost proposition but then reacquiring that customer either through ads at low cost or through email and other means will bump up your ultimate ultimately bump up your profitability improve your lifetime value and give you far more scope to reinvest in your growth as well

## [06:05 - 08:02]

so use catalog manager based on your type of products if you if you've got a single product store then catalog manager isn't really going to give you too much but certainly when you have multiple products going think about how you can kind of combine product sets so you know we created one here for an event you might do it based on categories etc etc is really down to you to define how you do that and the other thing about the catalog is you can also serve this to your top of funnel ads as well especially using instant experiences in collection ads it's a format that I really really like because it allows you to have your ad copy have a real nice kind of video as a kind of main main impact to get people to stop scrolling and then give them a scrollable list of items as well so for example if I was to use this Mother's Day collection top of funnel I would do my targeting you know try and find people that are interested in buying gifts or things for Mother's Day have a real cool video and some copy as the kind of main portion of that ad and then below pull in my collection of Mother's Day items so the way that would appear you know this is a mobile format is that you would have the big video and the content piece at the top and you have a scrollable list of products below that which you can see in the link below in the portion below as an example of how that would be presented it's a great way of introducing a cold audience to your brand and also getting them to the product fast without them having to go through your page land on your page look at products etc and it's far cheaper because you're leaving them on Facebook to scroll and engage in your collection your instant experience ad which aids lower CPMs and higher profitability for you from the ad as well.
